inline_comments.message: i think that for our purpose it should be set to 'need'
please don't use asterisk (*) in imports
please don't use asterisk (*) in imports
please don't use asterisk (*) in imports
What about ENV variables, that we have discussed, here? All 4 settings should be provided using ENV variables
Such should be read from ENV variables, as agreed
Different passwords to keystore and truststore
Why CERT_PASSWORD is used also here?
I propose to introduce new exception for TLS exceptions
Are we going to use JKS, not PKCS12? Any reason to use legacy solution?

Unacceptable! We are the owners of the certificate generation so hostname verification should be turned on.

BTW: generated certificate should have CN=aaf-cert-service-service or SAN=aaf-cert-service-service
It's old patch, I had made it before our discussion
It was not specified actually, I used jks, because Pawel Kasperkiewiecz provided to me in such form. Should we use PKCS12?
As far as I understand it, it requires the password to keystore. Which is CERT_PASSWORD.
Can I use the next available ExitCode (9) for that Exception?
I remember that last time we discussed that all endpoints should be disabled while only needed (e.g. health) should be enabled. Why this has changes since then?
Please log also whole exception to not loose root cause.

Would be good to not loose whole exception, so please add constructor which takes as input String and Exception :) And return here:

throw new TlsConfigurationException("TLS configuration exception: " + e, e);
There is no such constructor in ExitableException class
Cause you have access to truststore you should use it instead of using -k option
Cause you have access to truststore you should use it instead of using -k option
Cause you have access to truststore you should use it instead of using -k option

keys
Generate certService's client private and public keys
Cause this is an end entity certificate it should not have ca:true
Generate certificate signing request for certService's client
Sign certService's client certificate by root CA
Generate certService private and public keys
The CN must match K8s service name for CertService, so without .com if I remember correctly
No CA - but end entity certificate - remove ca:true
Generate certificate signing request for certService
Generate certificate signing request for certService
Sign certService certificate by root CA
Sign certService certificate by root CA
pls extract to separate private method createSslContext
should follow UpperCamelCase rules, could by, for example: SslContextFactory
should follow UpperCamelCase rules, could by for example: SslContextFactoryTest

Deployment should provide ENV variables: KEYSTORE_PATH, KEYSTORE_PASSWORD, TRUSTSTORE_PATH, TRUSTSTORE_PASSWORD. But such shouldn't be hardcoded but provided by values from helm chart, e.g. PATHs should be prefixed with  .Values.tls.server.volume.mountPath
Command should rely on ENV variables provided by deployment for both - keystore and truststore and password
Command should rely on ENV variables provided by deployment for both - keystore and truststore and password
Just wondering if both root-keystore.jks and truststore.jks are needed
we use truststore.jks in aaf-certservice-api and aaf-certservice-client.

You right, I forget about those ENVS. I will add them, but I have a question about " e.g. PATHs should be prefixed with  .Values.tls.server.volume.mountPath".
If I understand correctly, you want me to declare those 4 envs (for keystores and paths) here and get value for them from values.yaml file, right? 
But in that case I cannot use prefix (.Values.tls.server.volume.mountPath), becacuse Helm doesn't support templating in values.yaml file => i cannot use values to declare other values in same file.

We store only .jks format in ENV variables. And here we use root.crt and .p12 format. 
So I see 2 options:
1. Leave it as it is (just change pass to use ENV)
2. Add 2 more ENV Variables (for root.crt and certServiceServer-keystore.p12)

Which one you prefer?

I meant that if you provide KEYSTORE_PATH it should be like this {{Values.tls.server.volume.mountPath}}/keystore.jks. 
You don't need to provide in helm chart values for every path. I don't mind to hardcode names of files on deployment layer. 

BTW: I was aware that templating in values.yaml is not possible. Nokia Software (NSW) has its own plugin for helm to do this.
In deployment please provide two new ENV vars, but prefix them with mountPoint variable :) I don't mind to hardcode names of files on deployment layer.
My comment was related to unnecessary root-keystore.jks creation. Why we cannot in step 1 create truststore.jks, export from it root.crt and remove step_3 completly?
We need root-keystore.jks to sign another certificate for example in step 6 or step 11.
Ok, thanks for checking. Now I understand this :)
